Troubleshooting¶

This page covers common error conditions and how to resolve them.

Installation Issues¶

CMake Cannot Find FLINT¶

CMake Error: Could not find FLINT.

Install FLINT for your platform:

# macOS
brew install flint

# Ubuntu/Debian
sudo apt-get install libflint-dev

# RHEL/Fedora
sudo yum install flint-devel

If FLINT is installed in a non-standard location, set CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH:

pip install . --config-settings="cmake.args=-DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH=/opt/flint"

OpenMP Not Found (macOS)¶

On macOS, the system Clang does not include OpenMP by default. Install the libomp runtime via Homebrew:

brew install libomp

Then reinstall the package:

pip install --force-reinstall .

fk_main Binary Not Found¶

FileNotFoundError: fk_main binary not found

The compiled binary is missing from fkcompute/_bin/. This usually means the C++ build step failed silently during pip install.

Check the build log:

pip install . -v 2>&1 | grep -i "error\|warning\|cmake"

Then rebuild manually:

cmake -B build -S .
cmake --build build
cp build/fk_main src/fkcompute/_bin/fk_main

Gurobi Errors¶

No Gurobi Licence Found¶

gurobipy.GurobiError: No Gurobi license found.

If you did not install the optional ILP dependency, install it first:

pip install ".[ilp]"

Set the GRB_LICENSE_FILE environment variable to the path of your gurobi.lic file:

export GRB_LICENSE_FILE=/home/user/gurobi.lic

Add this to your shell profile for persistence.

If you do not yet have a licence, Gurobi offers two free options that are both sufficient for typical fkcompute computations:

  • Limited free licence — no registration required; covers the constraint sizes generated by fkcompute for most knots and links at moderate degrees. Obtain it via grbgetkey after installing Gurobi, or from the Gurobi free trial page.

  • Academic licence — full-size, unrestricted; free for faculty, students, and staff at degree-granting institutions. Apply at the Gurobi academic programme page.

Gurobi Licence Has Expired¶

Renew your licence at the Gurobi website and replace the gurobi.lic file.

Computation Errors¶

Inversion Search Returns No Result¶

InversionResult(success=False, ...)

The sign-assignment search did not find a valid assignment at the given degree. This can happen when:

  1. The degree is too low for the constraint system to be satisfiable. Try increasing the degree.

  2. The search space was limited by max_shifts or max_workers=1. Run with more workers and no shift limit:

    result = fk(braid, degree, max_workers=8, max_shifts=None, include_flip=True)

  3. The braid is not a fibered knot at this degree — some invariants are only defined above a threshold degree.

To inspect what was tried:

from fkcompute.inversion.api import find_sign_assignment

inv = find_sign_assignment(braid, degree, max_workers=4, verbose=True)
print(inv.success, inv.braid_type)

ILP Infeasibility¶

Gurobi: Infeasible

The constraint system generated from the inversion data has no integer solution. This usually means the sign assignment found in Phase 1 is not compatible with the degree. Possible causes:

  • The inversion was loaded from a file generated at a different degree. Make sure inversion_data matches the current degree.

  • A manually supplied inversion is incorrect. Verify the sign values.

Memory Errors in the C++ Backend¶

For very high degrees (≥ 10) with many-strand braids, the C++ backend may exhaust memory. Mitigations:

  • Reduce the number of threads (reduces peak memory from parallel buffers).

  • Run on a machine with more RAM.

  • Use intermediate save points: save the ILP file, then run the binary separately with ulimit -v to diagnose memory usage.

SymPy Not Available¶

ImportError: SymPy is required for symbolic output functionality.

Install SymPy:

pip install sympy
# or
pip install "fkcompute[symbolic]"

YAML Not Available¶

ImportError: PyYAML is required to load .yaml config files.

Install PyYAML:

pip install PyYAML
# or
pip install "fkcompute[yaml]"

Large Coefficient Strings¶

ValueError: Coefficient too large for symbolic computation: 12000 digits.

Very high-degree computations produce coefficients with thousands of digits that SymPy cannot handle in a reasonable time. Options:

  • Use the raw result["terms"] data directly.

  • Work numerically: evaluate the polynomial at specific values of \(q\).

  • Reduce the degree.

Verbose Logging¶

Enable verbose logging to see per-phase progress:

result = fk(braid, degree, verbose=True)

fk simple "[1,-2,1,-2]" 3 --verbose

Log messages are prefixed with [DEBUG] or [WARNING] and written to stderr.

Reporting Bugs¶

Please report issues at the project issue tracker, including:

  • The exact braid word and degree.

  • The full error traceback.

  • Output of pip show fkcompute and python --version.

  • Platform and OS version.
